scaled_oracle_cases.description: Replay the canonical main() seed flow for parse_exchange_file with valid flux bounds (should not raise ValueError).
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Prove that the function correctly parses valid TSV and YAML files without raising ValueError for valid bounds.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must raise ValueError if any parsed entry has lower > upper, but all entries here satisfy lower <= upper.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed behavior (without validation) would work; new behavior also works since bounds are valid.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
tsv_path = 'exchange_limits.tsv'
with open(tsv_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('compound\tlower\tupper\n')
    f.write('Glucose\t-10\t100\n')
    f.write('Lactate\t0\t50\n')
print('Parsing TSV file:')
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
for entry in parse_exchange_file(tsv_path, default_compartment='c'):
    print(entry)
yaml_path = 'exchange_limits.yaml'
with open(yaml_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('Glucose:-10:100:cytosol\n')
    f.write('O2:-1000:1000:m'+"""itochondria\n""")
print('\nParsing YAML file:')
for entry in parse_exchange_file(yaml_path, default_compartment='c'):
    print(entry)
result = None
covered_requirements: Add a constraint that each parsed entry must satisfy lower <= upper, raising a ValueError if violated.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Feed a TSV file with an entry where lower > upper and expect ValueError to be raised.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Prove that the function validates flux bounds and rejects entries with lower > upper.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must raise ValueError if any parsed entry has lower > upper.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed behavior would yield the entry without validation, so it would pass through invalid bounds.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
import tempfile, os
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.tsv', delete=False) as f:
    f.write('compound\tlower\tupper\n')
    f.write('BadMet\t10\t0\n')  # lower > upper
    f.write('GoodMet\t-5\t5\n')
    path = f.name
covered_requirements: Add a constraint that each parsed entry must satisfy lower <= upper, raising a ValueError if violated.
Scaled executable cases must include at least one case with invalid flux bounds that triggers a ValueError.
